Title: Deletion of GhSCY2D Causes Impaired Chloroplast Development and Temperature‐Dependent Leaf Yellowing in Cotton (Gossypium hirsutum L.)

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SCY2, component of chloroplast Sec2 translocon/system
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion or silencing of GhSCY2D causes temperature-sensitive leaf yellowing with reduced chlorophyll and carotenoid accumulation; overexpression rescues green leaf color.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A 10.327 kb deletion on chromosome D12 in the mutant removes GH_D12G0047 and GH_D12G0048, but functional tests identified GH_D12G0047 (GhSCY2D) as the causal gene for yellow leaves. 2. VIGS silencing of GhSCY2D in wild type induced leaf yellowing and reduced chlorophyll/carotenoid contents, while complementation/overexpression in the mutant restored green leaves. 3. GhSCY2D localizes to chloroplasts and is required for proper chloroplast ultrastructure, especially under lower temperature. 4. Loss of GhSCY2D disrupts chloroplast homeostasis and associates with impaired jasmonate-related recovery under low temperature.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Map-based cloning; genome resequencing; comparative transcriptomics; VIGS silencing; transgenic complementation/overexpression; subcellular localization; pigment measurement; TEM
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Temperature sensitivity at 25-28°C versus 32°C; exogenous MeJA treatment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seed and leaf
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher functional importance in leaves; mutant phenotype apparent at low temperature; expression rescued by complementation
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss of function reduces photosynthetic rate, causes ROS accumulation, shorter plant height, impaired chloroplast development
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (NCBI SRA: PRJNA1138104; PRJNA1138319); BSA-seq/resequencing accession NA; hormone metabolite profiling; flavonoid metabolomics
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S promoter used for subcellular localization construct
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Overexpression of GhSCY2D may help maintain photosynthetic rate under high temperature or intense light and could be used for high-light-efficiency breeding.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GhSCY2D is classified here as a common gene because it functions as a chloroplast protein transport/integration component rather than a biosynthetic enzyme or transcriptional regulator. The mutant lacks embryo lethality, possibly due to compensation by homoeolog GhSCY2A.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SecA2 component of chloroplast Sec2 system
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast inner membrane Sec2 system inferred
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing GhSECA2 causes etiolated/yellowing phenotype with reduced chlorophyll and carotenoid contents.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH_A02G1232 was identified as the cotton ortholog of Arabidopsis SECA2. 2. GhSECA2 interacts with GhSCY2D by AlphaFold3 prediction, Co-IP, and yeast two-hybrid assays. 3. VIGS silencing of GhSECA2 caused leaf etiolation and reduced chlorophyll/carotenoid contents, supporting its role in the same chloroplast homeostasis process as GhSCY2D.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog identification; AlphaFold3 structure prediction; Co-IP; yeast two-hybrid; VIGS silencing; pigment measurement
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing impairs chloroplast development and photosynthetic pigment accumulation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for preserving chloroplast integrity and photosynthetic efficiency, likely best considered together with GhSCY2D.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functional evidence indicates participation in the same Sec2 supercomplex/process as GhSCY2D; not identified as the primary causal mutation but experimentally validated in Results.

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SecE2 component of chloroplast Sec2 system
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Chloroplast inner membrane Sec2 system inferred
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Silencing GhSECE2 causes etiolated/yellowing phenotype with reduced chlorophyll and carotenoid contents.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GH_D02G2469 was identified as the cotton ortholog of Arabidopsis SECE2. 2. GhSECE2 interacts with GhSCY2D by AlphaFold3 prediction, Co-IP, and yeast two-hybrid assays. 3. VIGS silencing of GhSECE2 caused leaf etiolation and reduced chlorophyll/carotenoid contents, supporting its role in the same chloroplast homeostasis process as GhSCY2D.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Ortholog identification; AlphaFold3 structure prediction; Co-IP; yeast two-hybrid; VIGS silencing; pigment measurement
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Silencing impairs chloroplast development and photosynthetic pigment accumulation
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for chloroplast integrity engineering in combination with other Sec2 components.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functionally validated as a Sec2-system component in the same biological process as GhSCY2D.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: jasmonic acid-related chloroplast homeostasis; chlorophyll; carotenoids
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: supports chloroplast homeostasis and pigment maintenance under high temperature
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SCY2 homoeolog, component of chloroplast Sec2 system
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High temperature upregulates GhSCY2A, likely partially compensating for loss of GhSCY2D and contributing to recovery of green leaf color.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GhSCY2A is the homoeolog of GhSCY2D and its promoter contains a DRE element absent from the GhSCY2D promoter. 2. GhSCY2A expression was significantly higher at 32°C than at 28°C in the mutant. 3. The paper proposes that increased GhSCY2A expression at high temperature helps restore Sec2 function and chloroplast structure, thereby contributing to chlorophyll recovery.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter cis-element analysis; transcriptome/database expression analysis; qRT-PCR/inference from temperature comparison
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by extreme temperatures (4°C and 37°C) according to database analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in the mutant at 32°C than 28°C
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: May serve as a compensatory target for improving temperature resilience of chloroplast function.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a core common gene because it is directly discussed in Results as a temperature-responsive homoeolog contributing to phenotype recovery, though not functionally knocked out or overexpressed in this study.
